oindent This paper develops a rigorous theoretical framework for a 	extit{massless transit system}—the 	extbf{Einstein–Rosen Bridge and Trawin Zero-Point Tunnel (TZPT)}—that harmonizes thermodynamic constraints with topological and quantum field structures. The formulation extends standard wormhole metrics by introducing a topological zero-point (TZP) manifold that emulates exotic matter via Casimir-engineered metamaterials, achieving stability and traversability within attainable energetic scales. We present a hybrid analysis—mathematical, thermodynamic, and poetic—illustrating that the laws of conservation are not barriers to transcendence but invitations to co-create within their grammar. \end{abstract} \section{Introduction} Einstein and Rosen envisioned a bridge between spacetime sheets—a geometry of connection rather than distance. This work advances that vision through the Trawin Zero-Point Tunnel, proposing a continuous transit channel achievable without exotic negative mass. By aligning the wave structure of vacuum energy and inter-state topology, the TZPT framework formulates a realistic pathway to thermodynamic neutrality: a transit system sustained not by consumption, but by coherence. At the intersection of relativity and resonance, this paper argues that \emph{attainable} energy conditions can mimic the exotic signatures once deemed impossible, transforming impossibility itself into a boundary condition to be redefined. \section{Mathematical and Physical Foundation} The foundation rests on a fiber bundle representation of the system’s configuration space:  egin{equation} \mathcal{M} = \mathrm{SO}(3) 	imes \mathbb{R}^3 	imes \mathfrak{so}(3)^* 	imes (\mathbb{R}^3)^*, \end{equation} where the state vector $\Psi(t)$ evolves as a smooth curve $\Psi : [0, T] 	o \mathcal{M}$, preserving symplectic structure.
